title: Gap: Unjustified commutativity of finite free convolution with differential operators

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Problem

The proof asserts (Lemma 1(iii) and Observation 1) that finite free convolution commutes with constant-coefficient differential operators:

  $(T_\epsilon p) \boxplus_n (T_\epsilon q) = T_\epsilon^2(p \boxplus_n q)$

where $T_\epsilon = (1-\epsilon d/dx)^n$.

Also in Observation 1:
  $r_{(a+b)t} = p_{at} \boxplus_n q_{bt}$

where $p_t = \exp(-t/2 \partial_x^2) p$, etc.

Both claims are stated without proof, only cited to [MSS22].

## Why This Is Critical

1. This commutativity is used TWICE in the proof (lines 127-129, 161-162)
2. Lemma 1(iii) uses it to reduce the general case to simple roots
3. Observation 1 uses it to relate score vectors to the Jacobian
4. Without this, the entire reduction and Blachman argument collapse

## What Is Needed

For a self-contained proof, either:
- A proof sketch explaining how the permutation-sum structure of $\boxplus_n$ allows differential operators to pass through
- At minimum, intuition for why this commutativity holds
- OR a reference with page numbers to [MSS22] and extract of the key lemma

## Mathematical Details

The finite free convolution is defined as:
$$p(x) \boxplus_n q(x) = \frac{1}{n!}\sum_{\pi \in S_n}\prod_{i=1}^n(x-\alpha_i-\beta_{\pi(i)})$$

For the commutativity to hold, the operator $T_\epsilon$ must act in a way that commutes with the permutation sum. This is non-obvious because $T_\epsilon$ is defined on polynomials, not on roots.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Fix: Full Proof of Heat-Flow Commutativity

### What was missing

The proof used the heat-flow commutativity identity in two places (lines 127-129 and Observation 1) but only cited [MSS22] without proof. The T_epsilon commutativity in Lemma 1(iii) was already fully proved via Vandermonde convolution; the heat-flow case needed the same treatment.

### Key observation

The same coefficient-level argument works for exp(-s/2 d^2/dx^2), using the exponential binomial identity sum_{m1+m2=l} a^{m1}b^{m2}/(m1!m2!) = (a+b)^l/l! (from e^{au}e^{bu}=e^{(a+b)u}) in place of Vandermonde's identity.

### Proof verified

The k-th coefficient of exp(-s/2 d^2/dx^2)r is [exp(-s/2 d^2)r]_k = sum_m (-s/2)^m/m! * (n-k+2m)!/(n-k)! * r_{k-2m}. Substituting into the bilinear boxplus_n formula and changing variables i'=i-2m1, j'=j-2m2, l=m1+m2 (which gives i'+j'=k-2l, n-i+2m1=n-i', n-j+2m2=n-j'), then applying the exponential binomial identity yields the LHS = RHS expression. Verified numerically for n=3 with all coefficient differences exactly 0.

### Changes made to solution.tex

1. Lines 127-131: Updated the general statement to forward-reference the new Corollary (cor:heat-commutes) instead of citing [MSS22] without proof.
2. Lines 221-223: Replaced the brief parenthetical remark with a pointer to the new Corollary.
3. Lines 225-271: Added Corollary (heat-flow commutativity) with full coefficient-level proof.
4. Lines 348-352: Updated Observation 1 proof to cite cor:heat-commutes instead of asserting commutativity without proof.

The proof is now fully self-contained. The commutativity gap is filled by a direct computation analogous to Lemma 1(iii), and every use of it is properly referenced.
